title: Phase 11: Camoufox Config, SEO/AEO/GEO Commands & Skills
overview: Surface all 19 camoufox-js launch options through browse.json config and named profiles with a --camoufox-profile CLI flag. Add 3 convenience read commands (schema, meta, headings). Create 5 skills: browse-config (guided config generator), browse-stealth (rewrite), browse-seo, browse-aeo, browse-geo.
scopeChallenge: The camoufox runtime currently hardcodes 3 of 19 available launch options (headless, humanize, enable_cache). Critical stealth options like geoip, os, locale, block_webrtc are not exposed — making the fingerprint inconsistent with the proxy IP/locale. The fix is a camoufox section in browse.json + named profiles, not 19 env vars. The SEO/AEO/GEO workflows are already possible with existing commands but benefit from 3 convenience read commands. The 5 skills are documentation only.

prerequisites.verification: src/config.ts — BrowseConfig type, loadConfig() reads browse.json from project root

prerequisites.assumption: Camoufox resolver calls launchOptions() with hardcoded opts
prerequisites.verification: src/engine/resolver.ts:174 — { headless: true, humanize: true, enable_cache: true }

prerequisites.assumption: Command registry supports adding read commands

nonGoals: Individual env vars per camoufox option — use browse.json and profiles instead
Selector configs for specific engines — the LLM reads the snapshot

contracts.shape: CamoufoxConfig { geoip?, os?, locale?, blockImages?, blockWebrtc?, blockWebgl?, disableCoop?, humanize?, enableCache?, mainWorldEval?, proxy?, addons?, fonts?, customFontsOnly?, screen?, window?, fingerprint?, ffVersion?, firefoxUserPrefs?, debug? }
contracts.consistencyRule: Config values passed directly to camoufox-js launchOptions(). CLI flag --camoufox-profile loads a named profile that merges into/overrides the browse.json section.

failureModes.risk: Invalid camoufox config crashes browser launch
failureModes.mitigation: Wrap launchOptions() call in try/catch. Log which config key caused the error. Fall back to defaults.
Document in CamoufoxConfig JSDoc that 'virtual' headless mode requires Linux with Xvfb. On macOS/Windows, camoufox-js throws — the try/catch fallback handles it.
Clear error: 'Camoufox profile not found: .browse/profiles/<name>.json'. List available profiles.

failureModes.risk: New commands conflict with existing names
failureModes.mitigation: Verified: no existing commands named schema, meta, or headings in registry.ts

failureModes.risk: Skill guidance too vague for agent execution
failureModes.mitigation: Every skill step includes exact browse commands. Pattern from browse/SKILL.md.

testCoverageMap.codepath: Camoufox config from browse.json passed to launchOptions()
Named profile loaded and merged with browse.json defaults
--camoufox-profile flag parsed and forwarded to server
schema returns empty on page with no structured data
Camoufox launchOptions() called with full mapped config (skipIf)

tasks.title: Add camoufox config section to BrowseConfig and resolver
acceptanceCriteria: A browse.json with { "camoufox": { "geoip": true, "os": "windows" } } causes launchOptions() to receive those values
Missing camoufox section falls back to current defaults (humanize: true, enable_cache: true)
Invalid config key logs a warning but doesn't crash — browser still launches with valid options

tasks.title: Named camoufox profiles and --camoufox-profile flag
tasks.description:
Add `--camoufox-profile <name>` CLI flag in `src/cli.ts` (alongside existing flags like --runtime, --headed). The flag value is forwarded to the server via `BROWSE_CAMOUFOX_PROFILE` env var (same pattern as BROWSE_RUNTIME). The resolver's `loadCamoufoxConfig()` (from TASK-001) already reads this env var and loads the profile.

The CLI flag sets the env var on server spawn: `env.BROWSE_CAMOUFOX_PROFILE = cliFlags.camoufoxProfile`. No changes to server.ts needed — the resolver reads the env var directly.

Also add a `browse profiles` meta command that lists available profiles by scanning `.browse/profiles/*.json`. Register in `src/automation/registry.ts` as a meta command, implement in `src/commands/meta/profile.ts` (or inline in system.ts).

If the profile file doesn't exist at resolve time, `loadCamoufoxConfig()` throws a clear error listing available profiles.
acceptanceCriteria: browse --camoufox-profile stealth-google loads .browse/profiles/stealth-google.json and merges with browse.json
Missing profile file produces clear error with list of available profiles
browse profiles lists all .json files in .browse/profiles/ with their key settings

tasks.title: Register schema, meta, and headings read commands
tasks.description:
Add 3 new read commands to `src/automation/registry.ts` and implement them in `src/commands/read.ts`.

**schema** — extract structured data (JSON-LD, Microdata, RDFa) from the current page via page.evaluate(). Return JSON. If nothing found, return '(no structured data found)'.

**meta** — extract all SEO-relevant meta tags via page.evaluate(). Return structured text, one line per tag. Missing tags omitted.

**headings** — extract H1-H6 hierarchy via page.evaluate(). Return indented tree with count summary.

All three: safeToRetry: true, pageContent: true, MCP schemas via registry mcp field.

Also update CLI help text in `src/cli.ts` to list the 3 new commands under Read.
acceptanceCriteria: browse schema on a page with JSON-LD returns parsed structured data
browse meta on a page returns title, description, canonical, OG tags as structured text
browse headings on a page with no headings returns '(no headings found)'

tasks.title: Tests for new commands and camoufox config
tasks.description:
Create `test/seo-commands.test.ts` with tests for schema, meta, headings commands. Create `test/fixtures/seo-page.html` fixture with JSON-LD, meta tags, heading hierarchy, Microdata.

Also add camoufox config unit tests to verify:
- CamoufoxConfig keys map correctly to camoufox-js snake_case
- Profile loading merges with browse.json defaults
- Missing profile produces clear error

Register fixture in `test/test-server.ts`.
acceptanceCriteria: All schema/meta/headings test cases pass against HTML fixture
Config mapping test verifies camelCase → snake_case conversion for all 19 options
Profile merge test verifies profile values override browse.json defaults
Integration test (test.skipIf(!camoufoxAvailable)) calls launchOptions() with mapped config and verifies no error — skips gracefully when camoufox-js not installed

tasks.title: Create browse-config skill
tasks.description:
Create `skill/browse-config/SKILL.md` — a guided flow that asks the user questions and generates a browse.json camoufox section or a named profile.

Structure:
- Frontmatter: name browse-config, description, allowed-tools [AskUserQuestion, Bash, Read, Write], argument-hint [new config or profile name], when_to_use
- Step 0: Check if browse.json exists. If not, create one.
- Step 1: Ask what they're doing (stealth browsing, scraping, SEO audit, general)
- Step 2: Ask about proxy (none, have one, need recommendation)
- Step 3: Based on answers, generate the right config:
  - Stealth browsing: geoip=true, blockWebrtc=true, humanize=true, os='windows'
  - Fast scraping: blockImages=true, humanize=false, enableCache=false
  - SEO audit: geoip=true, humanize=true, enableCache=true
  - General: defaults
- Step 4: Ask if they want a named profile or project default
  - Named profile → write to .browse/profiles/<name>.json
  - Project default → write/update browse.json camoufox section
- Step 5: Show the generated config and how to use it
  - Profile: `browse --runtime camoufox --camoufox-profile <name> goto ...`
  - Default: `browse --runtime camoufox goto ...` (reads browse.json automatically)

The skill uses AskUserQuestion for the guided flow. It writes real JSON files. Referenced by browse-stealth as 'run /browse-config to set up your stealth profile'.
acceptanceCriteria: Skill uses AskUserQuestion to walk through config choices
Generated browse.json or profile JSON is valid and contains the right camoufox options for the selected use case
Skill shows the exact CLI command to use the generated config

tasks.title: Rewrite browse-stealth skill
tasks.description:
Full rewrite of `skill/browse-stealth/SKILL.md`. References /browse-config for initial setup instead of explaining config options directly.

Structure:
- Frontmatter: name, version 2.0.0, description, allowed-tools, argument-hint, when_to_use
- Step 0: Setup — 'Run /browse-config to generate your stealth profile, or set BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox'
- Step 1: Navigate with anti-detection (--runtime camoufox --headed, --camoufox-profile if configured)
- Step 2: Handle Cloudflare Turnstile (find widget, mouse movement, click, verify token)
- Step 3: Handle Google blocks (detect block, rotate session, retry with proxy)
- Step 4: Authenticated sessions (cookie import, profile mode)
- Step 5: Proxy rotation patterns
- What Camoufox spoofs (full C++ list)
- Limitations

Every step has exact browse commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Step 0 references /browse-config for setup
Every step includes exact browse CLI commands
Turnstile bypass includes mouse movement and token verification

tasks.title: Create browse-seo skill
tasks.description:
Create `skill/browse-seo/SKILL.md` — thorough SEO audit workflow using browse commands.

Covers: meta tags, headings, structured data, performance, links, mobile, images. Uses new schema/meta/headings commands. Recommends camoufox for bot-protected sites, references /browse-config for setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Complete SEO audit workflow covering meta, headings, schema, perf, links, mobile, images
Every step references exact browse commands including new schema/meta/headings
References /browse-config for camoufox setup

tasks.title: Create browse-aeo skill
tasks.description: Create `skill/browse-aeo/SKILL.md` — AEO page audit + SERP analysis. Two modes: audit a page for AEO readiness (schema, headings, meta, answer density), or analyze a Google SERP for AI features (snapshot -i, agent interprets @refs). References /browse-config for stealth setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers audit mode (page) and SERP mode (search results)
SERP mode explains that the agent interprets snapshot @refs — no magic parsing
References /browse-config for camoufox setup

tasks.title: Create browse-geo skill
tasks.description: Create `skill/browse-geo/SKILL.md` — GEO monitoring across Google AI Overviews, Perplexity, ChatGPT Search. Covers single-engine query, multi-engine comparison, domain monitoring. Exact navigation commands per engine. Recommends camoufox + --profile for authenticated sessions. References /browse-config for setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers Google, Perplexity, ChatGPT Search with exact navigation commands
Multi-engine comparison produces structured domain visibility report
References /browse-config for camoufox setup and --profile for authenticated engines
